page: Amazon Web Services c8a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 32 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache per core. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top 10% for single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3773.56 and a Geekbench single-core score of 2821.0. Multi-core performance is also strong, ranking in the top 25% with a stress-ng multi-core score of 120278.88. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached read-only small block bandwidth reaching 6812.8 GB/sec in the top 10% tier and uncached large block read bandwidth at 217.54 GB/sec in the top 25% tier. Cryptographic and vector execution capabilities are supported by AVX-512 and SHA_NI instruction sets, contributing to top-tier performance in database operations and static web serving throughput.

The instance offers high compute density and memory speed, making it a cost-efficient option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage. However, the lack of local storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, and the absence of a GPU limits its utility for heavy parallel training tasks. It is highly suited for workloads such as software compilation, text and image processing, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference, where it achieves 43.62 tokens/sec for text generation using a 7B model.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 15 Gbps baseline network speed. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data places its single-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth in the top 10% tier. It also demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core and uncached memory write tests. This hardware profile is optimized for CPU-intensive tasks such as software compilation, image processing, database operations, and small-to-medium LLM inference, offering a balanced, compute-dense architecture without the overhead of local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 15 Gbps network baseline, delivering top-tier single-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.8xlarge features 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor technology for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance featuring DDR5 memory and top-tier single-core performance for intensive CPU workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz.
64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz.
Top-tier single-core CPU and cached memory bandwidth performance.
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the Nitro hypervisor.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile.
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and LLM inference.
